claims.summary: Repeated sodium butyrate treatment reversed LPS-induced immobility in the forced swim test in mice.
Sodium butyrate reduced hippocampal cytokines and Iba1-positive microglial activation in LPS-treated mice.
A high-fiber inulin diet reduces age-related neuroinflammation in mice by promoting an anti-inflammatory microglial profile.
Pharmacologic butyrate treatment attenuates expression of IL-1β, TNF, and IL-6 in microglia from aged mice after LPS challenge.
Clostridium butyricum treatment decreased microglial activation in APP/PS1 Alzheimer mice, consistent with reduced neuroinflammation through a butyrate-mediated gut-brain mechanism.
Clostridium butyricum treatment suppressed microglial activation in the brain of APP/PS1 Alzheimer's disease mice.
Butyrate pretreatment reduced NF-κB p65 phosphorylation in BV2 microglia, indicating suppression of inflammatory signaling.
Sodium butyrate exacerbated neuroinflammation in an MPTP Parkinson mouse model, alongside worsening broader Parkinson-related pathology.
Sodium butyrate reduced neuroinflammation in 5XFAD mice and improved synaptic plasticity.

claims.outcome: hypothalamic microgliosis and inflammatory cytokine expression
claims.summary: Oral sodium butyrate reduces overnutrition-induced hypothalamic microgliosis and inflammatory cytokine expression in high-fat-diet mice.
Sodium butyrate supplementation prevents ethanol-induced increases in pro-inflammatory cytokine expression and normalizes microglial changes in the hippocampus of mice.
Sodium butyrate reduces neuroinflammation in chronic alcohol-exposed mice by suppressing microglial activation and lowering pro-inflammatory cytokines.
Sodium butyrate improves gut barrier integrity in chronic alcohol-exposed mice, as evidenced by increased occludin expression and reduced endotoxemia.
Sodium butyrate improves cognitive and mood-related outcomes in chronic alcohol-exposed mice.

claims.outcome: motor performance and dopaminergic neuron survival
claims.summary: Sodium butyrate improves motor performance and reduces dopaminergic neuron loss in MPTP-induced Parkinson's disease mice.
Sodium butyrate suppresses the TLR4/MyD88/NF-κB pathway across the gut-brain axis, contributing to neuroprotection in MPTP-induced Parkinson's disease mice.
Sodium butyrate reduced infarct volume in a diabetic-stroke mouse model.
Sodium butyrate improved long-term neurological recovery in diabetic-stroke mice.
Sodium butyrate amplified regulatory T cells in brain and blood, contributing to anti-inflammatory effects.
Sodium butyrate reduced prefrontal-cortex neuroinflammation in DSS ulcerative-colitis mice while also improving intestinal barrier integrity.
Sodium butyrate protects the prefrontal cortex from neuroinflammation in ulcerative colitis mice, as indicated by reduced IBA-1 levels.
Sodium butyrate activates Nrf2 and HIF-1α pathways, leading to upregulation of antioxidant enzymes such as catalase and superoxide dismutase.
Sodium butyrate alleviated lead-induced neuroinflammation and improved cognition in a lead neurotoxicity context.
Oral sodium butyrate improves learning and memory deficits in lead-exposed mice.
Sodium butyrate improved neurological scores in cardiac arrest mice.
Sodium butyrate reduced M1 microglia-associated neuroinflammation in cardiac arrest mice.
Sodium butyrate inhibited the TLR4/MyD88/NF-κB pathway, contributing to reduced neuroinflammation.

claims.outcome: neurologic injury after traumatic brain injury
claims.summary: Short-chain fatty acid supplementation after traumatic brain injury attenuates neurologic injury via the gut-brain-microglia axis.
Butyrate decreases uptake and accumulation of Aβ42 in endothelial cells and restores key blood-brain barrier functions under amyloid stress.

claims.intervention: diet engineered to increase microbial production of acetate and butyrate
claims.outcome: long-term neurologic impairment after traumatic brain injury
claims.summary: A diet engineered to increase microbial production of acetate and butyrate attenuates long-term neurologic impairment after traumatic brain injury.

experiment_proposals.title: Test whether administration_route, model_system, disease_state moderates the effect of sodium butyrate on neuroinflammation
experiment_proposals.rationale: Claims pmid_32556930::C1 and pmid_33785315::C1 may only disagree because context axes differ: administration_route, model_system, disease_state. A factorial design can separate true sign flips from hidden moderators.
design: Run a factorial intervention varying administration_route, model_system, disease_state alongside sodium butyrate vs control.
Measure neuroinflammation and any mechanistic mediators tied to ['colonic inflammation', 'neuroinflammation'].
Include enough power to detect interaction effects, not just main effects.
predictions: If claim pmid_32556930::C1 is right, sodium butyrate should increase neuroinflammation in context A.
If claim pmid_33785315::C1 is right, sodium butyrate should decrease neuroinflammation in context B.
If the moderator hypothesis is right, the effect sign should depend on the manipulated context axis rather than stay globally fixed.
